global_context.scene_description: A digital-painted cartoon illustration in the same style as the reference image — graphic-novel / Pixar-illustration painterly look, NOT photographic. The image is a vertical split-frame composition showing the SAME Phoenix family in two states: BEFORE (left half) and AFTER (right half). Same room, same characters, same furniture — drastically different conditions. LEFT HALF (BEFORE — the AC is dead): Husband sprawled on the couch in a sweat-soaked white tank top, towel on face, defeated. Wife mid-yell, pointing at the AC unit visible through the window, frustration clear on her face. The room is hot — orange-amber lighting, sweat visible everywhere, useless oscillating fan in corner, melting bag of ice on counter, sweat puddle on floor. Phoenix exterior visibly brutal heat outside. RIGHT HALF (AFTER — they finally called us, AC is fixed): SAME husband in same tank top now relaxed on the couch, beer in hand, head tilted back with a relieved laugh. SAME wife now sitting next to him, leaning into his shoulder, smiling, holding a coffee mug. The room is COOL — soft cool blue-white interior light, no sweat, no chaos, fan turned off, ice bag gone. Modern Nest thermostat on the wall reads 72°. Phoenix exterior still brutal outside the window — but it doesn't matter inside. A clean vertical line down the middle separates the two halves. Both halves are clearly the same illustrated style as the reference. The visual story is instant: dread to relief, all because they finally called. NO text overlay needed beyond a small label in a slim solid black bar at the very bottom of the frame, full-width, white text: 'BEFORE YOU CALL US >> AFTER YOU CALL US'. Cinematic illustrated style, NOT photo-realism.

lighting.source: left: harsh hot orange-amber | right: cool blue-white soft

composition.framing: vertical split-frame, hard center line, identical room on both sides
composition.focal_point: the contrast in body language — husband sprawled defeated on left, husband laughing relaxed on right
